response.reasoning: Bogdan has 20 years of engineering experience (exceeds 15-year requirement) with strong technical breadth across multiple domains and languages. His current role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, demonstrating relevant AI work. However, several gaps emerge: (1) his title is 'Staff Software Engineer,' not explicitly a director-level IC position; (2) his AI experience is primarily infrastructure/tooling-focused, not strategic products or platforms built with AI; (3) no Master's or PhD in AI field; (4) no clear evidence of strategic advisory work with enterprise customers on AI/cloud adoption; (5) entrepreneurial experience is via open-source (GISCollective), not startups within larger organizations. The foundation is solid but lacks the strategic positioning and demonstrated AI product expertise the role requires.
strengths: 20 years of engineering experience exceeds the 15-year requirement; breadth across full-stack, infrastructure, and embedded systems
Current role actively builds AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, providing directly relevant AI technology exposure
Demonstrated cloud competency (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) and multiple leadership roles (Staff Engineer) across organizations
concerns: No Master's or PhD in AI field; formal AI expertise is not demonstrated beyond current infrastructure work
Title is Staff Engineer, not explicitly director-level IC; unclear if experience includes strategic leadership and advisory responsibilities at required level
No demonstrated track record of shipping AI products or platforms; current AI work appears infrastructure/tooling-focused rather than strategic product-building
response.justification: Bogdan meets the baseline experience requirement and brings relevant current AI work plus strong technical foundation. However, the role specifically seeks director-level strategic leadership with recognized AI product expertise and enterprise advisory capability. His background is primarily deep technical IC work; his AI experience is infrastructure/tooling rather than strategic products or platforms. Without evidence of the strategic advisory and customer-engagement components central to the Principal Engineer brief, fit is uncertain despite solid technical credentials.
